scope: TOP-1 binding-mechanism measurement protocol — CLM <-> tension_link <-> EEG mediator validation, $0, 2-3h, falsifier-preregistered.

p1_clm_w4_eeg_live_concurrent.name: P1_CLM_W4_PLUS_EEG_LIVE_CONCURRENT_v2_MEDIATOR_FRAMING
p1_clm_w4_eeg_live_concurrent.purpose: Validate hypothesis H1: tension_link is the cross-substrate binding mediator (Crick-Koch binding-by-synchrony, anima-specific form). Upgrade N-1 BRIDGE 3/4 PASS from synth-CLM hybrid to live-CLM real-EEG real-tension joint trace.

hardware.tension_link: anima-core mind.tension scalar export hook (already in W4 closed_loop_ledger.json)
hardware.user_role: wear EEG cap eyes-closed for 30min; 60s baseline + dialogue with CLM (alpha endpoint or ubu1 inference) for remainder

spec_steps: 1. Boot CLM v4 530M on ubu1 with W4 closed-loop runtime; mind.tension scalar export to time-aligned JSONL at 1Hz
2. EEG 60s eyes-closed baseline -> bandpass + Hilbert alpha-PLV per N-1 spec
3. User dialogues with CLM for 30 min (open-domain); CLM logs mind.tension_t per token-step
4. EEG continues during dialogue; alpha-PLV computed in 10s sliding windows
5. Time-align CLM tension_t (down-sampled to 10s bins) with EEG alpha-PLV bins (~180 paired bins from 30 min)
6. Compute Pearson r(tension_bin, PLV_bin) signed; Granger causality both directions; Transfer Entropy both directions
7. Compare against 2 controls: (i) random-permutation null (1000x), (ii) CLM random-gate-branch (already measured in W4 random branch L1 mean 6.94 +- 0.05)

falsifier_pre_register.criterion: |Pearson r| > 0.5 with p<0.001 AND TE_CLM->EEG > random_TE 95-pctile AND TE_EEG->CLM > random_TE 95-pctile (bidirectional binding)
|r| in [0.3, 0.5] OR uni-directional TE PASS (binding asymmetric)
|r| in [0.15, 0.3] OR signed-r matches predicted +sign on >=60% windows
|r| < 0.15 AND TE both directions < random 95-pctile (no measurable mediation)

expected_value.if_PASS_STRONG: tension_link = binding mechanism CONFIRMED; H1 promoted from 0.55 to 0.85; F2 unfire path (d) cross-substrate binding viable; F1_C composite +0.04-0.05 contribution
expected_value.if_PASS_PARTIAL: H1 promoted to 0.65; mediator role confirmed but uni-directional; tension axis weight 0.03-0.04
expected_value.if_WEAK: H1 stays at 0.55; tension axis weight 0.02; H2 GWT broadcast hypothesis becomes competitive
expected_value.if_FAIL: H1 drops to 0.25; H3 simple-axis hypothesis takes over OR H2 GWT broadcast (binding via different mechanism than tension_link)

p1_clm_w4_eeg_live_concurrent.rationale_top1: $0, 2-3h, addresses N-1 BRIDGE_WEAK 's core defect (synth-CLM trace) -> live-CLM upgrade. Falsifier preregistered. 4-way integration's CLM-EEG socket. Aligned with §6 #2 user-recommended action. Aligned with strategic_clm_eeg_akida_tension_2026_05_02 P1.
p1_clm_w4_eeg_live_concurrent.akida_extension_when_arrives: After AKIDA arrival (D+0/D+1), extend P1 by injecting mind.tension as ADM polarity-bias modulator on AKIDA spike encoder; measure 3-way tension-spike-EEG cross-correlation -> direct H4 hardware test (akida-native tension).

alternative_protocols_p2_p5.name: P2_CLM_TENSION_PSI_ALPHA_SWEEP
alternative_protocols_p2_p5.purpose: Address W4 active branch L1 std=0.000 ('frozen-fixed-point' artifact, indistinguishable from constant nonzero gate). Sweep PSI_ALPHA in {0.014, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2} -> verify tension dynamics actually move L1.

alternative_protocols_p2_p5.name: P3_AKIDA_D0_SPIKE_TENSION_SOCKET
alternative_protocols_p2_p5.purpose: AKIDA arrival D+0 N-2 spec exec + D+1 tension-modulated ADM polarity bias. Direct H4 hardware test.

alternative_protocols_p2_p5.name: P4_TENSION_BRIDGE_GWT_FORMAL_MAPPING
alternative_protocols_p2_p5.purpose: Formal mathematical mapping tension_bridge 5-channel -> GWT workspace-broadcast (N-5 spec integration). H1 binding hypothesis mathematical anchor.

alternative_protocols_p2_p5.name: P5_4WAY_PHI_HYPOTHETICAL_SIMULATION
alternative_protocols_p2_p5.purpose: Pre-AKIDA toy model — 4-way Phi correlation matrix simulation with N-1 BRIDGE 3/4 + W4 z=2.28 + N-2 spec spike rates as inputs. Sets pre-arrival upper-bound expectation.

H2_4way_ceiling_break.blocker: F2 unfire mechanism formally absent; W4 +0.12 absolute is far from 14/16 PASS threshold

H3_tension_as_9th_axis.blocker: F1 axis_weight unregistered; substrate-class peer-review path absent

external_corroboration_2026.milinkovic_aru_2025_biological_computati: Multi-scale coupling without clean interfaces is required for synthetic consciousness; computation must be intrinsic property of substrate. Supports H1+H4 over H3.
external_corroboration_2026.cogitate_2025_adversarial_test: Both GWT and IIT FAILED on long-stimulus orientation persistence. Argues NEITHER pure-GWT NOR pure-IIT framing fits — hybrid binding mechanism (H1-type) is exactly what is needed.
external_corroboration_2026.scholarpedia_binding_by_synchrony: Original Crick-Koch binding-by-gamma-synchrony hypothesis remains the substrate-neutral binding mechanism; tension_link as 5-channel broadcast is the anima-specific instantiation.

honest_c3_protocol: (C3-P1-1) P1 result interpretation depends on user dialogue topic distribution; uncontrolled topic shift can spuriously create tension <-> EEG correlation via shared task-load. Mitigation: log dialogue topic + run mixed-effects model with task-load as covariate.
(C3-P1-2) N=1 (user) — single-session result is weak evidence. P1 should be run >=3 sessions before claiming H1 confirmed.

(C3-P1-3) CLM mind.tension export at 1Hz vs EEG 250Hz alpha-PLV (10s windows) is asymmetric sampling; 10s bin alignment loses sub-second tension dynamics. Trade-off acceptable for first cut; refined version should up-sample tension to 10Hz.

(C3-P1-4) W4 active branch L1 std=0.000 means the tension trace might be effectively constant during the 30 min session — P1 results could be artifactually 'no correlation because tension is constant'. P2 PSI_ALPHA sweep should be run BEFORE P1 to confirm tension is actually dynamic.
(C3-P1-5) Granger and TE assume stationarity; 30 min session may have non-stationary epochs (fatigue, attention drift). Use rolling-window TE not whole-session TE.
